quick_facts.label: Recruiting examination
quick_facts.value: Tax Assistant (CBDT) is listed among the Group C posts filled through the Staff Selection Commission’s Combined Graduate Level Examination (SSC CGL).

quick_facts.label: Employer and cadre
quick_facts.value: The post is in the Central Board of Direct Taxes (CBDT), Department of Revenue, Ministry of Finance.

quick_facts.label: Classification and age band
quick_facts.value: In the SSC CGL notice uploaded on May 21, 2026, Tax Assistant in CBDT is shown as a Group C post with an 18–27 age limit, subject to the notice’s eligibility date and applicable relaxations.

quick_facts.label: Education requirement
quick_facts.value: Tax Assistant falls under SSC CGL’s “all other posts” qualification: a bachelor’s degree from a recognized university or equivalent, with the qualification required by the notice’s specified cut-off date.

quick_facts.label: Pay-level context
quick_facts.value: The May 2026 SSC CGL notice places Tax Assistant (CBDT) at Pay Level 4, with the published basic-pay range of ₹25,500–₹81,100. This is a pay-matrix range, not a promise of a particular in-hand salary.

quick_facts.label: Skill-test requirement
quick_facts.value: SSC CGL Tier II includes a qualifying Data Entry Speed Test (DEST). SSC specifies a higher DEST standard for Tax Assistant in CBDT; the test is described as approximately 2,000 key depressions in 15 minutes.

sections.heading: Entry route: SSC CGL selection and post preference
paragraphs: The direct entry route is SSC CGL. The Commission’s 2026 notice lists Tax Assistant in CBDT as a likely Group C post and states that final allotment is based on merit-cum-preference of posts submitted by candidates. A candidate therefore needs to be eligible for SSC CGL, take the applicable computer-based tiers, and rank sufficiently for the CBDT Tax Assistant preference against vacancies reported for that cycle.

For this post, the general degree requirement in the cited notice is a bachelor’s degree or equivalent from a recognized institution. The same notice describes Tier I and Tier II as the examination structure. In Tier II Paper I, candidates must separately qualify its sections; the qualifying DEST is specifically relevant to Tax Assistant in CBDT and has a higher qualifying standard than for many other posts. Applicants should treat each future SSC CGL notice as controlling, because vacancy numbers, dates, cut-off date for age/qualification, and implementation details can change by recruitment year.
bullets: Obtain the required bachelor’s degree or equivalent by the cut-off date in the relevant SSC CGL notice.
Apply through SSC’s online process and complete the CGL computer-based examination stages.
Give Tax Assistant—CBDT an informed post preference when SSC calls for preferences.
Prepare for the higher-standard DEST as well as the objective portions of Tier II.

sections.heading: Skills signalled by the selection process
paragraphs: The official examination pattern makes keyboard accuracy, data entry, and baseline computer familiarity especially material for this role. Tier II Paper I contains Mathematical Abilities, Reasoning and General Intelligence, English Language and Comprehension, General Awareness, a Computer Knowledge Test, and DEST. The Commission makes DEST qualifying for all posts and identifies Tax Assistant in CBDT as one of the posts for which a higher DEST standard applies.

Practical preparation should therefore emphasize sustained accurate typing, careful handling of numeric and text data, basic computer concepts, clear written English, and disciplined checking of entries. These are evidence-based preparation priorities from the SSC selection design; they should not be read as a complete departmental job description.
bullets: Accurate keyboarding and data entry under a timed test.
Computer knowledge appropriate to SSC CGL Tier II.
Quantitative reasoning, English comprehension, general awareness, and logical reasoning.
Attention to detail when entering and checking text and numeric information.

sections.heading: Departmental setting and postings
paragraphs: A CBDT Tax Assistant is employed within the Income Tax Department’s direct-tax administration structure under the Department of Revenue, Ministry of Finance. The SSC notice identifies the cadre as CBDT rather than CBIC, which is a separate board and a separately listed Tax Assistant post.

Posting and personnel administration are managed within departmental regions and offices. For example, the Income Tax Department’s Kolkata regional site publishes Tax Assistant confirmation, seniority, transfer/posting, and promotion orders. This demonstrates that the cadre is administered through departmental establishments; a candidate should not assume a particular city or office from the SSC post title alone.
bullets: Employer: CBDT, Department of Revenue, Ministry of Finance.
Cadre: Group C Tax Assistant, distinct from Tax Assistant in CBIC.
Location and posting administration depend on departmental allocation and personnel orders.

sections.heading: Progression: departmental, vacancy-dependent
paragraphs: Career movement is governed by applicable recruitment rules, seniority, eligibility conditions, departmental processes, and vacancies rather than by an automatic time schedule. Departmental records from the Income Tax Department’s Kolkata region separately show Tax Assistant and Senior Tax Assistant seniority and promotion actions, as well as promotion actions for Income Tax Inspectors and Income Tax Officers.

A Central Administrative Tribunal matter concerning Income Tax Department employees describes individuals initially appointed as Tax Assistants after SSC CGL, later promoted to Senior Tax Assistant, and then promoted as Income Tax Inspectors after passing the departmental examination. This is evidence of a documented route, not a guarantee of timing or of promotion for every appointee. Candidates should check the rules and orders applicable to their own region and year of service.
bullets: Tax Assistant and Senior Tax Assistant are distinct departmental grades reflected in regional seniority and promotion records.
Progression to Income Tax Inspector has occurred through departmental promotion processes.
Promotion pace is variable and should not be inferred from any single region’s orders or individual case.

sections.heading: Compensation context
paragraphs: As of the SSC CGL notice uploaded on May 21, 2026, Tax Assistant in CBDT is shown at Pay Level 4: ₹25,500–₹81,100 in the published pay matrix. That figure is useful as a basic-pay context for the post, but it is not an annual package or take-home-pay estimate.

Actual monthly receipts can differ from the Level 4 basic figure because government pay is processed under applicable pay, allowance, tax, deduction, posting, and service rules. For an application decision, use the relevant year’s SSC notice for the pay level and consult appointment/pay documents for the terms that apply after allocation.
bullets: Dated reference point: SSC notice uploaded May 21, 2026.
Do not treat the range as a fixed in-hand salary.
